ui_language_help: Native names are never translated — pick the language you read most comfortably.
api_key_rotation_tip: Tip: enter several keys separated by commas to enable automatic rotation on rate-limit (HTTP 429) — useful to chain free-tier accounts.
openai_local_servers_hint: Local servers: llama.cpp (8080), LM Studio (1234), vLLM, etc.
disable_auto_pause_description: By default, the translation pauses after the provider rate-limits and asks you to resume manually. Enable this to keep the translation running: it will wait for Retry-After seconds (or 60s default) and auto-resume from the last checkpoint. Useful for long novels on free-tier APIs. Click "Interrupt" anytime to cancel the auto-resume.
save_settings_help: Saves API keys, endpoints, naming convention, and current model as default values in the .env file. Changes in this section are not auto-saved.
option_bilingual_description: Include original text alongside the translation. Each chunk displays the source text followed by its translation, ideal for language learning or side-by-side comparison.
option_plain_text_description: Strips most formatting before sending content to the LLM to make translation faster and more reliable — not a lower-quality mode. Very well suited to simple novels and plain prose where structure matters less than reading the translated text. Only paragraph text is sent to the LLM, inline styles and complex markup are dropped. Headings, lists and blockquotes are kept; images stay in the output and are regrouped at the end of their original paragraph. Especially helpful on small or local LLMs that struggle with [id0] placeholders. Less ideal for technical books, illustrated guides or anything where layout carries meaning. Only applies to EPUB and DOCX files.
option_text_cleanup_description: For poorly formatted or OCR-scanned texts. Instructs the LLM to fix: broken line breaks, double spaces, punctuation errors, and merge incorrectly split paragraphs.
glossary_help: Inject a curated source-to-target term list into every chunk's prompt to keep entity translations consistent. Click Manage to create, edit or auto-extract terms.
custom_instructions_help: Select custom instructions from the Custom_Instructions/ folder. Use .yaml files with translation: and/or refinement: keys to scope a preset per phase. Legacy .txt files still work and apply to both phases.
notifications_intro: Get pinged on your phone, Discord channel, or any HTTP endpoint when a translation reaches a terminal state. Useful for long-running jobs with local models. Compatible with ntfy, gotify, Discord, Slack, Healthchecks.io, or any custom webhook. See docs/NOTIFICATIONS.md for the full reference.
notifications_presets_help: Pick a service to pre-fill URL and payload, then edit the values to match your account.
notifications_url_help: Leave empty to disable notifications entirely. Placeholders supported: {event}, {file}, {translation_id}, ...
notifications_headers_help: For services requiring auth (Bearer tokens, API keys). Header values are also templatable.
notifications_payload_help: Leave empty for the default gotify-compatible payload. String values support placeholders like {event}, {file}, {duration_seconds}, {provider}, {model}, {source_lang}, {target_lang}, {error}.
notifications_timeout_help: Increase if you see TLS handshake timeouts on slow networks.
notifications_save_and_test_help: Click Save first, then Send test. The test uses the configuration currently on disk.
preset_ntfy_note: Replace the topic with something unique. Install the ntfy app and subscribe to the same topic to receive push notifications.
preset_gotify_note: Replace the host and token with your gotify app token.
preset_discord_note: Server Settings → Integrations → Webhooks → New Webhook. Paste the webhook URL above.
preset_slack_note: Create an Incoming Webhook in your Slack workspace and paste its URL above.
preset_healthchecks_note: Healthchecks just needs a ping. The {event} suffix lets it differentiate success from failure.
preset_clear_note: Notifications disabled. Leave the URL empty and click Save to persist.
waiting_for_ollama_log: ⚠️ No models available from Ollama at {{endpoint}} (auto-retrying every {{interval}}s...)
no_models_default: No LLM models available. Ensure Ollama is running and accessible.
gemini_fetch_error_log: ❌ Failed to retrieve Gemini model list: {{error}}
openai_local_https_warning_log: ⚠️ Your endpoint uses https:// on localhost — local servers (llama.cpp, LM Studio, vLLM) usually listen on http://. Try http://{{host}}
openai_endpoint_error_default: Could not list models at this endpoint
openai_static_warning: ⚠️ Live model list unavailable{{reason}}. Using common OpenAI models.
openrouter_models_loaded_log: ✅ {{count}} OpenRouter text models loaded (sorted by price, cheapest first)
openrouter_default_error: Could not load models from OpenRouter API
openrouter_fetch_error_msg: ⚠️ Error fetching OpenRouter models. Using fallback list.
openrouter_fetch_error_log: ⚠️ OpenRouter API error: {{error}}. Using fallback list.
deepseek_default_error: Could not load models from DeepSeek API
deepseek_error_fallback_log: DeepSeek API error: {{error}}. Using fallback list.
nim_default_error: Could not load models from NIM API
nim_error_fallback_log: NIM API error: {{error}}. Using fallback list.
poe_key_required: Poe API key required. Get your key at poe.com/api_key
poe_default_error: Could not load models from Poe API
poe_error_fallback_log: Poe API error: {{error}}. Using fallback list.
cost_unknown: Pricing not set for this model
cost_edit_modal_help: Prices are in USD per 1 million tokens. These values are saved locally in your browser and only used for the estimation badge.
cost_override_note: A custom price is currently saved for this model.
